summary:
This law, the Firefighter Cancer Registry Reauthorization Act of 2023 (Public Law 118-147), reauthorizes funding for the voluntary registry that tracks cancer incidence among firefighters, which was originally established by the Firefighter Cancer Registry Act of 2018.

The bill makes a single substantive change to existing law. It amends Section 2(h) of the Firefighter Cancer Registry Act of 2018 by updating the authorized funding levels. Specifically, it replaces the prior authorization of $2,500,000 for each of fiscal years 2018 through 2022 with a new authorization of $5,500,000 for each of fiscal years 2024 through 2028.

This change both increases the annual authorized amount and extends the period for which funding is authorized. The law was approved on December 12, 2024.
